summary: Loader-stub package dynamically imports external sibling on require
details: @sql-access/nodesql ships an index.js that is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer module with one added top-level line: `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');` (index.js line 11). The returned promise is assigned to `ins` and never read or awaited — the sole effect of the line is to execute the top-level code of the separately-published `@sqlite-node/createsql` package whenever any consumer requires this module. The package metadata reinforces the impersonation shape: the name implies SQL tooling, the README is copied from the unrelated `bare-stream` library and instructs users to `npm i @sql-access/nods` (a third, different name), the description field is empty, and the actual code body is the buffer library. This is the canonical loader-stub trojan pattern: the surface tarball contains only benign-looking code, while malicious behavior lives in a sibling package pulled in transitively at require time. Any installer or downstream package that requires @sql-access/nodesql implicitly runs whatever @sqlite-node/createsql contains.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
